message:
Refactor runtime to use scheduler-driven task execution with adaptive deadline monitoring

Replace the thread-per-task spawn model in RuntimeInner with a
ThreeLaneScheduler-backed approach: spawn() now creates a task in
RuntimeState and injects it into the scheduler, with worker threads
pulling work via run_once(). Drop gracefully shuts down the scheduler
and joins workers.

Expand deadline_monitor with adaptive per-task-type thresholds:
- DurationHistory collects completion times per task type
- Percentile-based warning thresholds (nearest-rank, configurable)
- Fallback threshold when sample count is below min_samples
- record_completion() feeds back durations from lab and runtime
- Refactored check() to separate mutable state scanning from
  side-effect emission (checkpoint, violation, warning metrics)
- New test coverage via TestMetrics implementing MetricsProvider

Simplify WorkStealingScheduler to a thin wrapper over ThreeLaneScheduler,
removing ~60 lines of manual LocalQueue/Stealer/Parker/Worker plumbing.
Expose ThreeLaneWorker::run_once() and helper methods as pub(crate).

Additional fixes:
- EpochBarrier: double-check inside write lock to prevent TOCTOU race
  between is_triggered() read and barrier trigger
- Epoll reactor: validate fd with fcntl(F_GETFD) before registration
  to catch stale/closed file descriptors early
- OTel: remove inline deadline_remaining histogram from deadline_warning()
  (now handled via record_completion); add metric method test coverage
- Lab runtime: call monitor.record_completion() after task finishes so
  adaptive thresholds learn from lab executions
- RuntimeState: add task(), task_mut(), region(), region_mut() accessors
- Test fixes: sync_e2e barrier test uses real threads instead of
  single-threaded lab (fixes deadlock), RwLock test uses try_read,
  lab_determinism accounts for arena reclamation, scope doctests add
  explicit require_send/require_static calls

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
